class.name: Marked (Natwuns)
class.proficiency: Constitution, Wisdom, Athletics, Arcana, History, Insight, Intimidation, Nature, Survival
class.tools: Tattooist's tools, one type of artisan's tools of your choice

feature.name: Starting Marked
feature.text: As you create a marked, consider the first steps you took on your journey and what compels you to keep walking this path. Did you learn of the marked from another member of this reclusive class, or through rare writings regarding its decorated alumni? What significant events influenced your decision to embrace this way of life, and how do they shape your outlook and approach to tattoo magic? What sets you apart from others who wield this immense power?

Perhaps you encountered a marked who took you under their wing, begrudgingly guiding you through the secrets of this oftentimes solitary journey. Maybe you’re driven by a desire for self-mastery and power, seeking to understand the mystic energies accumulating within you. Or perhaps you’re deeply drawn to the artistry of tattoo magic, treating your body as a living canvas and curating each tattoo with care.
Class Features

Proficiencies Armor: Light armor, Medium armor Weapons: Light armor, Medium armor Tools: Tattooist’s tools, one type of artisan’s tools of your choice. Saving Throws: Constitution, Wisdom Skills: Choose two from Choose two from Athletics, Arcana, History, Insight, Intimidation, Nature, and Survival

Equipment You start with the following equipment, in addition to the equipment granted by your background: • (a) a longsword or (b) any simple weapon • (a) a chain shirt or (b) leather armor • a diplomat’s pack • Two common magic tattoos of your choice, already applied and attuned • Tattooist’s tools
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Multiclassing and the Marked
feature.text: Ability Score Minimum. As a multiclass character, you must have at least a Constitution score of 13 to take a level in this class, or to take a level in another class if you are already a marked.
Proficiencies Gained. If the marked isn’t your initial class, you gain the following proficiencies when you take your first level as a Marked: light armor, medium armor, and tattooist’s tools.
Imprints. The marked’s Imprints are unique to this class and do not apply to other classes, even if those classes provide magic tattoos or similar abilities.

Magic Tattoos Obtained. Upon taking your first level as a marked, you gain two common magic tattoos of your choice. These tattoos are integral to the early levels of the marked, reflecting their initial connection to tattoo magic.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Imprints
feature.text: Starting at 1st level, each magic tattoo you attune to grants you 1 Imprint, representing both a physical and magical connection to every magic tattoo you apply. Imprints embody the permanent buildup of mystic energy that seeps within you, lingering as faint traces even after a tattoo is extracted. Every tattoo applied, attuned to, and eventually extracted, leaves a lasting Imprint that reflects your unique connection to tattoo magic, symbolizing the indelible bond between the marked and their inked power.
Imprints are counted in two ways: overall Imprints and active Imprints.

Overall Imprints: This is the cumulative number of all magic tattoos you have ever attuned to—a badge of honor representing your growth and journey as a marked. Each time you apply and attune to a new magic tattoo, your overall Imprints increase by 1, signifying your body’s deepening reservoir of mystical energy.

Active Imprints: Active Imprints represent the usable mystic energy drawn from your tattoos and expended to fuel certain abilities. The number of active Imprints starts off equal to your overall Imprints but can decrease as you expend them for features and abilities. Once expended, active Imprints are not easily regained, reflecting the finite reserve of mystic energy available within each magic tattoo.
The two common magic tattoos you receive at 1st level count toward your Imprints, giving you 2 overall Imprints and 2 active Imprints to start.
PASSIVE BONUSES: (IMPRINT THRESHOLDS)
As you amass Imprints, you gain passive bonuses that activate at certain active Imprint thresholds, representing the lingering mystic energy, vitality, and strength imbued by your magic tattoos:
• 2 Active Imprints – Bolstered Form: You gain a +1 bonus to AC. Your skin has become more resilient due to magical residue, making you harder to harm.
• 4 Active Imprints – Vital Ink: Your maximum hit points increase by 5. The vitality of your tattoos strengthens your lifeforce.
• 6 Active Imprints – Mystic Pulse: Your body reacts to magic more readily, granting you advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects.
• 8 Active Imprints – Sage Resilience: You gain resistance to a damage type of your choice, chosen at the start of each long rest.
• 10 Active Imprints – Empowered Strikes: Your melee and unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

These bonuses remain active as long as you have at least the required number of active Imprints for each threshold. For example, if you have accumulated 6 overall Imprints, you gain the benefits of Bolstered Form, Vital Ink, and Mystic Pulse. If your active Imprints drop below 6 (to 4), you lose the benefit of Mystic Pulse until your active Imprints are restored to 6 or higher.
TRACKING IMPRINTS

As a player, you’ll keep track of both your overall Imprints and your active Imprints. Overall Imprints reflect your lifetime accumulation of tattoos, while active Imprints represent your current pool of usable mystical energy. The reserve of active Imprints allows you to access the passive features of the marked class and activate powerful abilities in battle.

By steadily acquiring and attuning to new magical tattoos, your overall Imprints increase, granting you access to greater thresholds of power. This growth reflects your evolution as a living tapestry of tattoo magic, empowered by each tattoo that leaves its mark on your skin and spirit.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Soulflare
feature.text: Beginning at 3rd level, you can expend your active Imprints to create temporary surges of power drawn from the mystic energy within you. As a bonus action, you may expend up to 3 active Imprints to activate one of the following Soulflare effects. These effects last for 1 minute and can each be used once per long rest.
• Soul Guard: Expend 1 active Imprint to gain a +2 bonus to AC for the duration.
• Flaring Soul: Expend active 2 Imprints to regain hit points equal to your Marked level + Constitution modifier.
• Flaring Strikes: Expend 3 active Imprints to gain +1d8 bonus damage on all melee attacks.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Mystic Legacy
feature.text: At 3rd level, you delve into the advanced techniques of a tattooist style of your choice. Your choice grants you features at 5th level and again at 9th and 15th level.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ability Score Improvement
feature.text: When you reach 4th level, and again at the 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You can also forgo this feature and take a feat of your choice instead.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos

feature.name: Eternal Imprint
feature.text: At 5th level, your body and spirit have become so deeply entwined with tattoo magic that you can magically recall previously applied tattoos from your physical memory. Once per long rest, as an action, you may recall one magic tattoo of common or uncommon rarity that you have previously attuned to and instantly reapply it to yourself, provided you have an available attunement slot. The tattoo reappears on your skin, as if freshly applied.

The recalled tattoo is fully functional and remains until you end attunement and extract it. When this tattoo is removed through standard extraction, its design vanishes immediately from the needle, making it unrecoverable until you summon it again with Eternal Imprint.

Additionally, this recalled tattoo grants you 1 active Imprint upon reattunement. However, recalled tattoos do not contribute to your overall Imprint count, ensuring that the permanent accumulation of Imprints reflects only newly acquired tattoos, not those summoned with Eternal Imprint.
You are also unable to recall a magic tattoo that is currently applied to you.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Anchored Ink
feature.text: At 7th level, your mastery allows you to retain even more mystic energy. You can now attune to one additional magic tattoo without it counting toward your regular magic item attunement cap, further cementing your role as a true conduit of magic tattoos.

In addition, whenever you expend active Imprints for Soulflare or other class abilities, roll a d4. On a roll of 3 or 4, you regain 1 expended Imprint as you attempt to anchor the expended energy of your tattoos.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ability Score Improvement
feature.text: When you reach 4th level, and again at the 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You can also forgo this feature and take a feat of your choice instead.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos

feature.name: Ink in my veins
feature.text: At 9th level, you’ve mastered the practice of drawing on the amassed power stored within your Imprints, fortifying your body with their essence. When you drop to half your hit points or lower, you may use your reaction to awaken the latent power within your tattoos. You gain temporary access to all Imprint Threshold bonuses up to your total overall Imprints for 1 minute, regardless of how many active Imprints you currently have.
Once this feature is activated, you can choose to expend 1 active Imprint to maintain the effect for an additional minute per active Imprint expended, up to a maximum of 3 additional minutes.
You may use Ink in my Veins once per long rest, reflecting the powerful but taxing nature of tapping so deeply into your reserve of mystic energy.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ability Score Improvement
feature.text: When you reach 4th level, and again at the 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You can also forgo this feature and take a feat of your choice instead.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos

feature.name: Enduring Imprint
feature.text: At 13th level, your entanglement with tattoo magic has reached new heights, allowing you to recall and reapply tattoos of greater power. When you use Eternal Imprint, you may now recall one magic tattoo of rare rarity that you have previously applied and attuned to, instead of being limited to common or uncommon tattoos.

The recalled tattoo is fully functional, remaining until you end attunement and extract it, after which its design vanishes from the needle as usual, making it unrecoverable until you summon it again with Eternal Imprint.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Boundless Ink
feature.text: At 15th level, you have begun to surpass the physical limitations of the body, drawing on reserves of mystic energy that only a true conduit of tattoo magic could access. You can now attune to one additional magic tattoo, granting you a total of two additional attuned magic tattoos beyond your regular magic item attunement limit. This expanded capacity cements your status as a master of inked magic.

Additionally, whenever you expend active Imprints for Soulflare or other class abilities, you automatically regain 1 expended Imprint at the end of your turn, reflecting your peerless connection to the energies stored within you.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ability Score Improvement
feature.text: When you reach 4th level, and again at the 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You can also forgo this feature and take a feat of your choice instead.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos

feature.name: Soulstorm
feature.text: At 17th level, your mastery over manipulating Imprints reaches its zenith, transforming Soulflare into a more potent selection of power. When you activate Soulflare, you may choose from the following enhanced and additional options. Each effect reflects a heightened resonance and lasts for 1 minute. Each effect may be used once per long rest.
• Soul Guard: Expend 1 active Imprint to gain a +4 bonus to AC for the duration.

• Flaring Soul: Expend 2 active Imprints to regain hit points equal to twice your Marked level + your Constitution modifier. Each time you regain hit points during this minute, you also regain additional hit points equal to your proficiency bonus.
• Flaring Strikes: Expend 3 active Imprints to add +2d8 bonus force damage to all melee attacks.
• Mystic Surge: Expend 4 active Imprints to immediately regain one-quarter of your maximum hit points (rounded down) and double your movement speed for the duration.

• Imprint Explosion: Expend 5 active Imprints to unleash a powerful short-range area attack centered on you, forcing all creatures within 10 feet to make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, a creature takes 6d8 force damage and is knocked prone; on a successful save, it takes half damage and remains standing.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ability Score Improvement
feature.text: When you reach 4th level, and again at the 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can’t increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
You can also forgo this feature and take a feat of your choice instead.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos

feature.name: Living Archive
feature.text: At 20th level, you have become a true living archive of tattoo magic, with your body and spirit forever entwined with the inked power within you. You gain the following benefits:
EXTENDED IMPRINT THRESHOLDS
Your accumulation of Imprints continues to grant you powerful benefits beyond the 10 Imprint threshold:
• 12 Imprints – Godly Resilience: You gain immunity to one damage type of your choice, selected at the start of each long rest.
• 14 Imprints – Boosted Recovery: Whenever you roll hit dice to regain hit points, add an additional 10 hit points to the total recovered.
• 16 Imprints – Unstoppable: When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. This ability activates once per long rest.
INFINITE RECALL
When you use Eternal Imprint, you may now recall magic tattoos of very rare rarity in addition to common, uncommon, and rare tattoos.
LIMITLESS
You may now attune to one additional tattoo, granting you a total of three additional tattoos beyond your regular magic item attunement limit.
MORE INK IN MY VEINS

At 20th level, Ink in My Veins evolves into More Ink in my Veins, allowing you to channel the full might of your mystical energy in a moment of profound need. You now gain the following additional benefits whenever Ink in my Veins is activated:
• Mystic Overflow: At the end of each of your turns, you automatically regain 1 expended active Imprint, maintaining a continuous flow of mystical energy.

• Surging Power: All of your melee and spell attacks deal an additional +3d8 force damage as you channel the combined power of your accumulated tattoos into every strike. This bonus damage increases by 1d8 for every 10 overall Imprints you have, to a maximum of +6d8 force damage, reflecting the peerless strength you have built over your journey.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Mystic Legacy: Legacy of Iron - ntmtII
feature.text:
The Legacy of Iron embodies a marked's unwavering commitment to self-improvement. Every tattoo you bear reinforces your physical form like layers of metal, blending muscle and magic into an unbreakable fortress. Those who follow this legacy harness their ink as shields, drawing upon the raw, enduring power that comes with every mark. Over time, your body becomes as steadfast as the iron you emulate, able to withstand even the harshest blows.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ironclad Imprints (Legacy of Iron - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 3rd level, whenever you activate Soulflare, your skin marked by designs hardens, bolstered by the essence of your tattoos. You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your level in this class. These temporary hit points are in addition to any other effects that provide temporary hit points.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Smelted Defenses (Legacy of Iron - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 6th level, your tattoos fortify your defenses as though hammered from iron. When you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to roll a d6 and reduce the damage by the number rolled + your Constitution modifier. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Steelbound (Legacy of Iron - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 10th level, the bond between your tattoos and your spirit steels your resolve, making you a bastion on the battlefield. As a bonus action, you can gain resistance to non-magical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Forges Resilience (Legacy of Iron - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 14th level, the strength within your tattoos allows you to rise again, even when brought to death's door. When you drop to 0 hit points but are not killed outright, you can choose to drop to 1 hit point instead. Additionally, you immediately gain temporary hit points equal to your level in this class + your Constitution modifier. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Mystic Legacy: Legacy Of Living Ink - ntmtII
feature.text:
The Legacy of Living Ink is a path for those whose tattoos have started taking on a life of their own. Each design upon your flesh becomes a reservoir of untapped potential, adapting to amplify the marked's attacks, create new unique effects, and even shifting into forms that assist the marked in times of need. For a marked who follows this legacy, tattoos are not static but living, shifting entities that act as capable allies.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Branding (Legacy Of Living Ink - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 3rd level, your tattoos react dynamically to your will, pulsing with vibrant energy that enhances your strikes. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon or unarmed strike, you can expend 1 active Imprint to have a tattoo flare to life, dealing an additional 1d10 force damage. The magical damage may take on a physical appearance reflective of the tattoo's design during the attack, such as a fiery rune, wisps of shadowy tendrils, or an aura of radiant light.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Inkshift (Legacy Of Living Ink - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 6th level, your tattoos gain the ability to shift and adapt, transforming to enhance your offensive capabilities. As a bonus action, you can choose one of the following effects for 1 hour: • Pursuit Form: Your tattoos take on sharp, jagged, aggressive designs. When you deal damage to a creature, you can deal an extra 1d6 force damage to another creature within 5 feet of the original target. • Arcane Form: Your tattoos ripple with arcane energy, enabling you to cast the cantrip of a spell tattoo (such as fire bolt, chill touch, or shocking grasp) once per turn as part of your Attack action for the duration • Predatory Form: Your tattoos twist into beastlike, feral shapes, granting you advantage on attack rolls against creatures that are below onequarter of their maximum hit points, as your tattoos help you sense their weakness. Once you use this feature, you must complete a short or long rest before using it again.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Sentient Ink (Legacy Of Living Ink - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 10th level, your tattoos more willingly share their potency, surging with power to enhance you further. When you activate Ink in my Veins, you can choose to amplify one of your Imprint Threshold bonuses for the duration. Select one of the following enhancements:
• If you have Bolstered Form, your AC bonus increases to +2, as your tattoos fortify your defenses even further. • If you have Mystic Pulse, you can cast the shield spell once for the duration as a reaction without expending a spell slot. • If you have Empowered Strikes, you gain a bonus +1d8 force damage on all melee attacks. Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Self-Preserving Ink (Legacy Of Living Ink - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 14th level, your tattoos have evolved to the point where they can act autonomously, reacting to extreme danger with destructive force. When you drop to 0 hit points but are not killed outright, your tattoos come alive, emitting an arcane burst that affects creatures within a 15-foot radius. Each creature in that area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or take 3d10 force damage and be knocked back 10 feet.
After this burst, you regain hit points equal to half the total damage dealt by the arcane burst (rounded down), as the mystical energy stabilizes you. You can use this feature once per long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Mystic Legacy: Legacy of the Ancients - ntmtII
feature.text:
Those who follow the Legacy of the Ancients tap into age-old forces and spirits from forgotten times. The tattoos of these marked are enchanted with floating symbols of ancient guardians, mystical creatures, and otherworldly protectors. When they enter battle, these tattoos flare to life, channeling the strength and magic of ages long past to aid the marked in ways both subtle and spectacular.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Spectral Guardian (Legacy of the Ancients - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 3rd level, you can call upon the protective spirits of ancient guardians that once bore one of your marks. When a creature targets you with an attack, you can use your reaction to summon a Spectral Guardian that imposes disadvantage on the attack roll. This spectral form briefly appears as an ethereal figure that attempts to parry, then fades. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain all expended uses after a long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Bound Spirits (Legacy of the Ancients - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 6th level, your tattoos can manifest as wisps of spiritual energy that shield you and nearby allies. As a bonus action, you can summon a Bound Spirit—a protective spirit—that hovers within 10 feet of you for 1 minute. You gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks while the Bound Spirit absorbs incoming damage. While the Bound Spirit is active, you can use your reaction to have it intercept an attack targeting an ally within 10 feet of you, imposing disadvantage on the attack roll as the sphere rushes to protect them. You can use this feature once per short or long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Beastly Sentinels (Legacy of the Ancients - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 10th level, you gain the power to summon a spectral beastly spirit to defend you in moments of peril, as they had for their companions of old. When you activate Ink in my Veins, you can also call forth a Beastly Sentinel—a spectral creature resembling a powerful beast such as a lion, eagle, or wolf. The protector stays by your side for the duration of Ink in my Veins, offering both offensive and defensive boons. While the Beastly Sentinel is present, you gain the following benefits: • A Familiar Presence: The Beastly Sentinel creates a protective aura around you, reducing all incoming damage by an amount equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1) for you and one ally of your choice within 10 feet. • Spectral Strike: As a bonus action, you can direct the Beastly Sentinel to strike a creature within 15 feet of you, dealing 2d8 radiant or necrotic damage (your choice) as it lashes out with ethereal claws or talons. Once you use this feature, you must complete a long rest before you can use it again.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Ancient Avatar (Legacy of the Ancients - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 14th level, you unlock the ability to temporarily embody the essence of an ancient spirit. As an action, you can activate Ancient Avatar, transforming into a figure that once bore one of your marks, infused with energy amassed from your tattoos for 1 minute. While in this form, you gain the following benefits: • Incorporeal Strikes: Your melee attacks deal an additional 3d8 radiant damage. • Otherworldly Armor: You gain resistance to all damage except force damage, as your form partially shifts to another realm upon being attacked. You can also pass through creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. • Imposing Presence: Once per turn, you can use a bonus action to release a pulse of spectral energy, forcing all creatures within 10 feet of you to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or become frightened of you until the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you complete a long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Mystic Legacy: Legacy of the Tempest - ntmtII
feature.text:
Marked who follow the Legacy of the Tempest are avatars of the storm and sky, embodying the raw, relentless power of thunder and lightning. Their tattoos crackle with electrical energy, arcing and flashing as they channel the fury of the elements. Marked who follow the legacy of the tempest are feared for their ability to sweep through the battlefield and cause disastrous levels of destruction, striking with shocking speed and devastating force.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Stormbound Strikes (Legacy of the Tempest - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 3rd level, your connection to the storm enhances your attacks. When you make a melee weapon attack, you can channel lightning through your weapon to deal an extra 1d6 lightning damage to the target. Additionally, your movement speed increases by 10 feet after the attack as the power of the tempest flows through you. You may choose to add this bonus lightning damage a number of times per short or long rest equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Thunderous Reprisal (Legacy of the Tempest - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 6th level, you learn to call upon thunder to strike back at your behest. When a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction and unleash a thunderous shockwave. The attacking creature must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, the creature takes 2d8 thunder damage and is pushed back 10 feet. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't pushed. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Tempestuous (Legacy of the Tempest - ntmtII)
feature.text:
At 10th level, you radiate the power of a raging storm. As a bonus action, you can activate Tempestuous for 1 minute, which emits a swirling field of wind and lightning in a 10-foot radius around you. While your aura is active, you gain the following benefits: • Electric Surge: Any creature that starts its turn within the aura or enters it for the first time on a turn takes 1d6 lightning damage. • Unyielding Winds: Ranged weapon attacks targeting you or allies within your aura are made with disadvantage as fierce winds whip around you. • Swift Movement: Your movement speed increases by an additional 10 feet for the duration. You can use this feature once per short or long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II

feature.name: Eye of the Storm (Legacy of the Tempest - ntmtII)
feature.text:
(ENHANCED TEMPESTUOUS)
At 14th level, you unlock the ultimate power of the tempest, gaining the ability to become the embodiment of a storm. As an action, you can activate Eye of the Storm, transforming into a storm-wreathed form for 1 minute. While in this form, you gain all the effects of Tempestuous as well the following benefits: • Flight: You gain a flying speed of 30 feet as thunderclouds and lightning arcs carry you through the air. • Shocking Strikes: Your melee attacks deal an additional 2d8 lightning or thunder damage (your choice) as bolts of electricity and booming thunder accompany each strike. • Thunderous Burst: As a bonus action, you can emit a pulse of thunderous energy in a 15-footradius area you can see within 30 feet. Each creature in that area must make a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save, a creature takes 3d8 thunder damage, is pushed back 15 feet, and is knocked prone. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage, is not pushed back, and remains standing. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Natwuns' Tome of Mystical Tattoos II
